sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחְמֹד and יֶחְמַד, whence נֶחְמְדֵהוּ, Isa 53:2

(1) to desire, to covet, Exo 20:17 34:24 Mic 2:2.

(2) to delight in any thing, Psa 68:17 Isa 1:29 53:2 Pro 12:12, with the addition of a dat. of benefit לֹו Pro 1:22. Part. חָמוּד something to be desired, something desirable, hence that which is dearest to one, Job 20:20 Psa 39:12. חֲמוּדֵיהֶם Isa 44:9, “their delight,” i.e. idols (comp. Dan 11:37).

Niphal, participle נֶחְמָד-

(1) desirable, hence pleasant, agreeable, Gen 2:9 3:6 .

(2) precious, Psa 19:11 Pro 21:20.

Piel, i.q. Kal No. 1. Son 2:3, בְּצִלֹּו חִמַּדְתִּי וְיָשַׁבְתִּי “I desire to sit down in his shadow.” Lehrgeb. § 222, 1 note.

Hence מַחְמָד, מַחְמֹד, and the words immediately following.
